text:
To measure exactly 45 minutes using the ropes, which burn unevenly and take 60 minutes to burn completely, we can follow these steps:

1. Light one rope at both ends. Since it burns unevenly, we can't predict exactly how long it will take to burn, but we know it will burn twice as fast because it's lit at both ends. Thus, it will take 30 minutes to burn completely (60 minutes / 2).

2. At the same time, light the other rope at one end. This rope will burn for 60 minutes.

3. When the first rope, lit at both ends, finishes burning (after 30 minutes), 30 minutes have passed. The second rope has also been burning for 30 minutes, so it has 30 minutes of burn time left.

4. Now, light the second rope at the other end as well. Since it had 30 minutes of burn time left, lighting it at both ends means it will now burn twice as fast, taking 15 minutes to burn the remaining length.

5. Since 30 minutes have already passed (from the first rope burning) and the second rope takes an additional 15 minutes to burn after being lit at both ends, the total time elapsed is 30 + 15 = 45 minutes.

This method uses 2 ropes to measure exactly 45 minutes.
